title: butomaceae
intro: The Butomaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as water-plantains. This family consists of approximately 13 species of aquatic plants, primarily found in temperate regions of the world. Butomaceae plants are typically submerged or floating in water and have long, narrow leaves and showy flowers. They are often found in freshwater environments, such as ponds, lakes, and slow-moving streams.
